system_description.overview: Baba Care is an AI voice agent (companionship agent) specifically designed for seniors. The platform leverages LLMs to adapt the AI's speech to the senior's preferences and analyzes conversations post-completion. It helps seniors feel less lonely and helps care facilities save time on administrative tasks.

products.Outbound / Inbound Phone Calls: Users can call Baba or request a call from Baba on a scheduled basis. Users can speak with Baba for up to 60 minutes about simple conversational topics. Baba asks questions, offers trivia/fun facts, and provides emotional companionship.
products.Care Hub: B2B dashboard for care workers to track client usage patterns including pickup rate, total time spent chatting with Baba, etc.
products.SMS: Users receive generic SMS messages from Baba reminding them to call back if an inbound call goes to voicemail.

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out method - AWS controls excluded from scope)
regions: Not explicitly specified - hosted in high availability data centers with multiple availability zones
infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices or servers; 133 NE 2nd Ave, Apt 1107, Miami, FL (HQ address only)
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (explicitly stated: infrastructure hosted in high availability data centers with multiple availability zones; DR involves spinning up multiple servers across all availability zones)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: System hosted in AWS VPC with segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs). Network topology includes segmented VPCs protecting from unauthorized external access.
network_architecture.api_gateway: Application Load Balancer (ALB) with HTTPS (443) via ACM and TLS Termination (ACM Certificate)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS employed to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings. Firewalls and intrusion detection tools configured to prevent unauthorized access and detect external security threats.
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access done through a bastion host with a virtual private network (VPN).
network_architecture.tls: TLS encryption using certificates from an established third-party certificate authority. HTTPS (443) via ACM with TLS Termination at the ALB.
network_architecture.content_filtering: All systems restricted to internet with content filtering system routed through proxy server.
network_architecture.topology: User -> Route 53 (DNS) -> Application Load Balancer (HTTPS 443 via ACM, TLS Termination) -> VPC containing: ECS Services (Fargate) running FastAPI + Celery Worker + Celery Beat -> RDS Proxy -> Amazon RDS (database) + Redis 7 (ElastiCache) for caching/task queuing. AWS Secrets Manager provides env vars/secrets to ECS services.

application_architecture.pattern: Microservices (containerized services with task queue workers)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments (Stage and Production mentioned). Changes tested in separate environment before implementation.

data_storage.backup_frequency: Formal backup policies and procedures in place; specific frequency not disclosed

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS IAM / Password policy of AWS
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform. MFA required for source code tool access. Two-factor authentication required for email access.
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination of employment
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through AWS password policy - includes periodic forced changes, password expiry and complexity requirements. Accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts.

encryption.in_transit: TLS (version not specified) via ACM certificates
encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (AWS KMS implied)
encryption.key_management: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest and store/manage encryption keys. Access to production environment's access keys restricted to authorized individuals.

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Source code management tool with MFA access (specific tool not named - likely GitHub based on branch rules description)
ci_cd_devops.ci_cd_tool: Not explicitly named; internal tool tracks changes from initiation through deployment and validation
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to move changes to production requires additional approval. Developers cannot make changes to production without approval.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Infrastructure as code mentioned (baseline configurations retained within infrastructure as a code, configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management with monthly patch management reviews by CTO/Management. Workstations configured for automatic updates. Daily patch compliance scanning. OS patches managed and applied as they become available.

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not explicitly named; monitoring tools used for system capacity and performance monitoring
monitoring_logging.siem: Not explicitly named; automated alerts for high and critical risk events
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled to monitor activities (administrative, logon attempts, data deletions, changes to functions, security configurations, permissions, roles) at application and infrastructure level
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events. Monitoring tool generates alerts when predefined thresholds are met.

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure and data center services (subservice organization)
AI/LLM capabilities for the voice agent conversational features

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed on all production servers (continuous automatic scanning, weekly definition updates). Also installed on Mac-based workstations and laptops.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications (frequency adjusted as required). Self-assessment scan prior to every release or yearly.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party. High/critical findings remediated in timely manner.

bcdr.rto: Not disclosed (time frames identified in DR plan based on business impact analysis but not stated in report)

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access controls: AWS responsible for data center physical security; not in scope
A1.2 - Environmental protections: Babacare does not have an office; environmental controls managed by building management/AWS
Processing Integrity - Determined not relevant to the system
Privacy - Determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC8.1.2 - Branch protection enforced: developers cannot make changes to production code without additional approval via merge request
CC8.1.3 - Source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed to author with MFA-protected access to source code tool
CC6.1.1 - MFA enforced for administrative access to production platform
CC6.6.1 - Firewalls and intrusion detection tools configured on application gateway and production network
CC7.4.1 - BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually with testing of different loss scenarios
CC4.1.1 - Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
CC7.1.2 - Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with remediation plan for critical/high findings
CC9.2.1 - Vendor management policy with annual SOC report reviews and quarterly risk assessments for vendors without SOC reports
CC6.2.2 - Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including admin privileges

infrastructure.rationale: AWS with Multi-AZ, VPC, ALB, ECS Fargate, RDS Proxy are solid choices. IaC mentioned but tool not named. No CDN, no multi-region, no specific region disclosed. Good use of managed services (Fargate, ElastiCache, RDS Proxy) but limited detail on redundancy specifics.

application_architecture.rationale: Containerized microservices pattern with FastAPI + Celery workers on ECS Fargate is a modern, solid architecture. Branch protection and code review enforced. However, only one language/framework evident, no mention of testing frameworks, no GraphQL/gRPC sophistication. API style is standard REST.

data_layer.rationale: RDS (via RDS Proxy) + Redis 7 ElastiCache is a reasonable setup with caching layer. Multi-AZ confirmed. Data classification and retention policies exist. Backup policy exists but frequency not disclosed. No data warehouse, no S3/file storage mentioned, no specifics on database engine.

security_posture.rationale: Strong security controls: MFA on production and source code, IDS/IPS, firewalls, annual pen testing, monthly vulnerability scanning, endpoint protection with AV/anti-malware, server hardening, encryption at rest and in transit, quarterly access reviews, 1-day access revocation SLA. Missing: no WAF mentioned, no named SIEM tool, no cybersecurity insurance. VPN + bastion host for remote access is good.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection enforced, separate environments confirmed, IaC mentioned, emergency change process exists, formal change management policy. However, specific CI/CD tool not named, IaC tool not named, container orchestration is Fargate (managed). Monthly patch reviews are decent. Lacks specificity on deployment automation.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually. Annual testing of DR plans with different loss scenarios. Multi-AZ failover with ability to spin up across all AZs. Formal backup policy. However, RTO/RPO not disclosed in report, no multi-region failover, backup frequency not specified.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Heavy single-vendor dependency on AWS for all infrastructure (compute, database, caching, secrets, DNS, load balancing, certificates). Only subservice org mentioned is AWS. LLM provider is unnamed but implied. Very low vendor diversity creates significant concentration risk.

overall.rationale: Babacare demonstrates a reasonably mature security posture for an early-stage startup with a SOC 2 Type 1. The architecture is modern (containerized, managed services) but the report lacks specificity on many tools and configurations. Single-vendor AWS dependency is a risk. No WAF, no named SIEM/APM, no disclosed RTO/RPO, and Type 1 (point-in-time, no operational effectiveness testing) are limitations. Good fundamentals but needs maturation.

red_flags: No WAF (Web Application Firewall) mentioned despite being a web-facing application handling sensitive senior/healthcare data
Heavy single-vendor AWS dependency with no multi-region capability
No specific SIEM tool named - unclear if centralized security monitoring exists beyond basic AWS logging
SOC 2 Type 1 only (point-in-time design review, no operational effectiveness testing over a period)
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria excluded despite handling personal data of seniors
Very small team structure (CEO + Engineering Team + Growth Team) creates key-person risk
yellow_flags: RTO/RPO not disclosed despite having BCDR policy - unclear recovery time commitments
Backup frequency not specified in report
CI/CD tool not named - unclear deployment automation maturity
IaC tool not named despite claiming infrastructure as code
No S3 or file storage mentioned - unclear where call recordings/conversation data is stored
No specific database engine mentioned (just 'Amazon RDS')
LLM provider not named as subservice organization despite being critical to core functionality
No mention of data encryption key rotation policy
Audit firm (Accorp Partners) is a smaller/less well-known firm - PAC-FIRM-LIC-47383
green_flags: Modern containerized architecture: ECS Fargate + FastAPI + Celery + Redis ElastiCache + RDS Proxy
MFA enforced on production access, source code, and email
Branch protection with mandatory code review for production merges
Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with remediation plan
Quarterly user access reviews including admin accounts
One business day access revocation SLA upon termination
VPN + bastion host for remote access
Formal change management with emergency change process
BCDR tested annually with different loss scenarios
Vendor management policy with annual SOC report reviews
Data retention and disposal policy in place
Network diagram provided showing clear architecture
Server hardening with baseline configurations managed as code
key_observations: The network diagram reveals a clean, well-structured architecture: Route 53 -> ALB -> ECS Fargate (FastAPI + Celery Worker/Beat) -> RDS via Proxy + Redis 7 ElastiCache, all within a VPC with AWS Secrets Manager
For an AI voice agent handling senior care data, the exclusion of Privacy trust criteria is notable and could be a concern for healthcare-focused customers
The org chart shows only CEO with Engineering Team and Growth Team reporting to them - very lean organization typical of early-stage startup

The report mentions 'Windows/Network team' for patch management which seems inconsistent with a cloud-native startup using Mac workstations and AWS - suggests some boilerplate language from the audit firm
Celery Beat + Worker pattern indicates scheduled outbound calls and async task processing, consistent with the product's scheduled call feature
RDS Proxy usage is a sophisticated choice for connection pooling, suggesting engineering maturity beyond the team size
Content filtering via proxy server mentioned but no specific tool named - unusual for a fully cloud-native startup
The report was prepared very quickly (audit date July 24, 2025; assertion date August 19, 2025) suggesting urgency, likely for customer/enterprise sales requirements
